text: Understand and act in accordance with The Code: Professional standards of practice and behaviour for nurses and midwives (NMC 2018)
Apply relevant legal, regulatory and governance requirements, policies and ethical frameworks to all areas of practice, differentiating between devolved UK legislatures where appropriate
Understand and apply the principles of courage, transparency and the duty of candour, recognising and reporting situations, behaviours or errors that could result in poor care outcomes
Demonstrate an understanding of and the ability to challenge discriminatory behaviour
Develop, manage and maintain appropriate professional relationships with people, their families, carers and colleagues
Understand the professional responsibility to adopt a healthy lifestyle to support physical and mental wellbeing
Demonstrate an understanding of research and evidence-based practice and apply this to inform safe and effective nursing practice
Demonstrate the knowledge, skills and ability to think critically when applying evidence and drawing on experience to make evidence-informed decisions in all situations
Understand the need to base all decisions regarding care and interventions on person-centred approaches that respect and value individual diversity
Demonstrate resilience and emotional intelligence and be capable of explaining the rationale that influences judgments and decisions in routine, complex and challenging situations
Communicate effectively using a range of skills and strategies with colleagues and people at all stages of life and with a range of mental, physical, cognitive and behavioural health challenges
Demonstrate an understanding of the relationship between mental and physical health and demonstrate equitable approaches to practice in relation to all mental and physical health issues
Demonstrate the skills and abilities required to develop, manage and maintain appropriate therapeutic relationships with people, their families and carers
Demonstrate that people's rights, autonomy and confidentiality are respected and upheld in accordance with relevant legislation and ethical frameworks
Demonstrate the ability to keep up to date with emerging evidence and demonstrate a commitment to continued professional development
Demonstrate the ability to keep complete, clear, accurate and timely records
Take responsibility for continuous professional development, learning from experience and seeking challenge
Demonstrate the knowledge and confidence to contribute effectively and proactively in an interdisciplinary team
Act as an ambassador for the nursing profession, upholding the reputation of nursing and promoting public confidence in nursing, health and care services
Safely and effectively apply evidence-based practice across all fields and settings

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Understand and apply the aims and principles of health promotion, protection and improvement and the prevention of ill health when engaging with people

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Demonstrate knowledge of epidemiology, demography, genomics and the wider determinants of health, illness and wellbeing

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Understand and apply strategies to promote and improve health and wellbeing and to prevent ill health

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Identify and use all appropriate opportunities to discuss the impact of smoking, substance and alcohol use, sexual behaviours, diet and exercise on mental, physical and behavioural health

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Promote and improve health by understanding and explaining the principles, practice and evidence base for health screening programmes

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Understand the importance of early years and childhood experiences and the possible impact on life choices, mental, physical and behavioural health and wellbeing

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Understand and explain the contribution of social influences, health literacy, individual circumstances, behaviours and lifestyle choices to mental, physical and behavioural health outcomes

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Explain and demonstrate the use of up-to-date approaches to behaviour change to enable people to use their strengths and make informed choices when managing their own health

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Use appropriate communication skills and strength-based approaches to support and enable people to make informed choices about their care to manage health challenges

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Understand the principles of epidemiology and contribute to population health needs assessment

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Promote health and prevent ill health by understanding and explaining the principles of pathogenesis, immunology and the evidence base for immunisation, vaccination and herd immunity

platformTitle: Promoting health and preventing ill health
text: Apply health promotion principles in community and public health settings
Demonstrate and apply knowledge of human development from conception to death, including body systems, homeostasis and physical and behavioural sciences, when assessing needs
Undertake holistic and systematic assessment of people's physical, psychological, social, spiritual and emotional needs
Demonstrate and apply knowledge of all commonly encountered mental, physical, behavioural and cognitive health conditions when undertaking nursing assessments and developing care plans
Understand and apply a person-centred approach to nursing care, demonstrating shared assessment, planning, decision making and goal setting
Demonstrate the ability to accurately process assessment information to identify nursing care needs and develop person-centred, evidence-based care plans
Effectively assess a person's capacity to make decisions about their own care and to give or withhold consent
Understand and apply the principles and processes for making reasonable adjustments and best interest decisions where people do not have capacity
Recognise and assess signs and symptoms of mental health deterioration or distress
Undertake routine nursing health and risk assessments
Interpret results from routine investigations, taking prompt action when required by implementing appropriate interventions, requesting additional investigations or escalating to others
Undertake risk assessments in a range of settings using appropriate assessment tools
Identify and assess the needs of people and families for care at the end of life, including requirements for palliative care and decision making related to treatment and care preferences
Demonstrate the ability to work in partnership with people to continuously monitor, evaluate and reassess the effectiveness of nursing care plans and readjust goals
Demonstrate knowledge of when and how to refer people safely to other professionals or services for clinical intervention or support
Review and escalate care plans in response to changing clinical presentation or new assessment findings
Document assessment findings, care plans and nursing decisions accurately and in a timely manner
Demonstrate and apply an understanding of what is important to people and how to use this knowledge to ensure their needs for nursing care are met
Work in partnership with people to encourage shared decision-making in order to support individuals, their families and carers to manage their own care when appropriate
Demonstrate the knowledge, communication and relationship management skills required to provide people, families and carers with accurate information before, during and after interventions
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills required to support people with commonly encountered mental health, behavioural, cognitive and learning challenges
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills required to support people with commonly encountered physical health conditions, their medication usage and treatments
Demonstrate the knowledge, skills and ability to provide evidence-based nursing care to meet people's needs related to nutrition, hydration and elimination
Demonstrate the knowledge, skills and ability to provide evidence-based, person-centred nursing care related to mobility, hygiene, oral care, wound care and skin integrity
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills required to identify and initiate appropriate interventions to support people with common symptoms including anxiety, confusion, discomfort and pain
Demonstrate the knowledge and skills to prioritise person-centred nursing care at end of life, including care for people who are dying, the deceased and the bereaved
Demonstrate the knowledge and ability to respond proactively and promptly to signs of deterioration or distress in mental, physical, cognitive and behavioural health
Demonstrate the ability to manage commonly encountered devices and confidently carry out related nursing procedures
Demonstrate knowledge and skills for recognising and responding to medical emergencies, providing first aid and basic life support
Understand the principles of safe and effective administration and optimisation of medicines and demonstrate proficiency and accuracy when calculating prescribed dosages
Demonstrate knowledge of pharmacology and the ability to recognise the effects of medicines, allergies, drug sensitivities, side effects, contraindications, adverse reactions and polypharmacy
Demonstrate knowledge of how prescriptions can be generated and the risks associated with generic, unlicensed and off-label prescribing
Apply knowledge of pharmacology to the care of people, demonstrating the ability to progress to a prescribing qualification following registration
Demonstrate the ability to co-ordinate and undertake the processes involved in routine planning and management of safe discharge home or transfer between care settings
Provide evidence-based nursing care and support to people with complex health and care needs, including those who are frail

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Understand the principles of effective leadership, management, group and organisational dynamics and culture and apply these to team working and decision making

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Understand and apply the principles of human factors, environmental factors and strength-based approaches when working in teams

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Understand and apply the principles of performance management and how these apply to the nursing team

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Demonstrate an understanding of the roles, responsibilities and scope of practice of all members of the nursing and interdisciplinary team

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Safely and effectively lead and manage the nursing care of a group of people, demonstrating appropriate prioritisation, delegation and assignment of care responsibilities

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Exhibit leadership potential by demonstrating an ability to guide, support and motivate individuals and interact confidently with other members of the care team

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Demonstrate the ability to monitor and evaluate the quality of care delivered by others in the team and lay carers

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Support and supervise students in the delivery of nursing care, promoting reflection, providing constructive feedback and evaluating their performance

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Demonstrate the ability to challenge and provide constructive feedback about care delivered by others in the team and support them to identify and agree individual learning needs

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Contribute to supervision and team reflection activities to promote improvements in practice and services

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Effectively and responsibly use a range of digital technologies to access, input, share and apply information and data within teams and between agencies

platformTitle: Leading and managing nursing care and working in teams
text: Understand the mechanisms that can be used to influence organisational change and public policy, demonstrating the development of political awareness and skills

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Understand and apply the principles of health and safety legislation and regulations and maintain safe work and care environments

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Understand the relationship between safe staffing levels, appropriate skills mix, safety and quality of care and escalate concerns appropriately

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Comply with local and national frameworks, legislation and regulations for assessing, managing and reporting risks, ensuring appropriate action is taken

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Demonstrate an understanding of improvement methodologies, participate in all stages of audit activity and identify appropriate quality improvement strategies

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Demonstrate the ability to accurately undertake risk assessments in a range of care settings using contemporary assessment and improvement tools

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Identify the need to make improvements and proactively respond to potential hazards that may affect the safety of people

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Understand how the quality and effectiveness of nursing care can be evaluated in practice and use service delivery evaluation and audit findings to bring about continuous improvement

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Demonstrate an understanding of how to identify, report and critically reflect on near misses, critical incidents, major incidents and serious adverse events

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Work with people, their families, carers and colleagues to develop effective improvement strategies for quality and safety

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Demonstrate an understanding of the differences between risk aversion and risk management and how to avoid compromising quality of care and health outcomes

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Acknowledge the need to accept and manage uncertainty and demonstrate an understanding of strategies that develop resilience in self and others

platformTitle: Improving safety and quality of care
text: Understand the role of registered nurses and other health and care professionals when managing and prioritising actions and care in the event of a major incident
Understand and apply the principles of partnership, collaboration and interagency working across all relevant sectors
Understand health legislation and current health and social care policies, and the mechanisms involved in influencing policy development and change
Understand the principles of health economics and their relevance to resource allocation in health and social care organisations and other agencies
Identify the implications of current health policy and future policy changes for nursing and other professions and understand the impact on delivery and coordination of care
Understand and recognise the need to respond to the challenges of providing safe, effective and person-centred nursing care for people who have co-morbidities and complex care needs
Demonstrate an understanding of the complexities of providing mental, cognitive, behavioural and physical care services across a wide range of integrated care settings
Understand how to monitor and evaluate the quality of people's experience of complex care
Understand the principles and processes involved in supporting people and families with a range of care needs to maintain optimal independence and avoid unnecessary interventions
Facilitate equitable access to healthcare for people who are vulnerable or have a disability and demonstrate the ability to advocate on their behalf and make reasonable adjustments to care
Understand and implement the principles and processes involved in planning and facilitating the safe discharge and transition of people between caseloads, settings and services
Demonstrate the ability to identify and manage risks and take proactive measures to improve the quality of care and services when needed
Understand the processes involved in developing a basic business case for additional care funding by applying knowledge of finance, resources and safe staffing levels

Demonstrate an understanding of the importance of exercising political awareness throughout their career to maximise the influence and effect of registered nursing on quality of care, patient safety and cost effectiveness
Communicate effectively using a range of verbal, non-verbal and written communication skills and strategies – Annexe A
Perform proficiently across all nursing procedures and clinical skills required for registration across all fields of practice – Annexe B
